title: Activity timeline — unified chronological feed of all project activity
description:
Dashboard view and CLI command showing a merged timeline of ALL activity within a timeframe:

DETERMINISTIC (no LLM):
- Bead created/updated/closed with who and reason
- Sessions started/ended with turn count, token usage, model
- Human input messages (from graph attention)  
- Agent runs dispatched with bead link, tool use count, result
- Git commits with message and file count
- Notes dropped with tags
- Graph ingestion events (new sources added)

Each entry tagged: human (blue), agent (green), system (gray).
Filterable by: timeframe (--last 2h, --today, --since DATE), project, type.

CLI: graph activity [--last 2h] [--project autonomy] [--type human]
Dashboard: /activity page with auto-refresh, linked entries (click bead/session/commit).

LLM SUMMARY (future, optional):
Feed the deterministic timeline into a prompt for narrative summary.
'What happened today? What progressed? What's stuck? What needs attention?'
This is a librarian job, not a dashboard feature.

title: Fix worktree mount — agent commits must persist on host branch
description: Agent committed d3112b5de4 inside container but commit doesn't exist on host. Worktree is mounted at /workspace/repo but agent's git commits are not persisting to the host filesystem. Need to debug: is the mount actually read-write? Is git inside the container writing to the mounted dir or a container-local copy? Is the .git link in the worktree surviving the mount?
notes: commit: 45b7969 fix: mount .git dir into container so worktree commits persist. Root cause: worktree .git file points to /home/jeremy/workspace/autonomy/.git/worktrees/<name> which didn't exist inside container. Fix: mount .git at same absolute path.
close_reason: Worktree .git reference resolved by mounting .git dir at same absolute path. Verified: commits from container persist on host branch.

title: graph read --json — structured source content for dashboard rendering
description: Add --json to graph read. Returns: {source: {id, type, title, project, platform, created_at, metadata}, entries: [{entry_type, role, turn_number, content, message_id, metadata}]}. The dashboard needs structured data to render type-aware layouts — notes vs conversations vs sessions vs docs all render differently. Also add graph edges --json for a source to return linked beads, parent sessions, entities.
close_reason: graph read --json returns {source: {id, type, title, project, platform, created_at, metadata}, entries: [{entry_type, role, turn_number, content, message_id, metadata}], edges: [...]}. Dashboard API updated to use --json. Tested with notes and sessions.

title: Source view rendering — proper layout for notes, conversations, sessions
description:
The source detail view needs type-aware rendering:

NOTES: Single card with content, metadata header (date, project, tags), no turn numbers.

CONVERSATIONS/SESSIONS: Chat-style layout — user messages left-aligned (blue border), assistant messages right-aligned (gray border). Role labels not turn numbers. Code blocks syntax highlighted. Matched search turn highlighted with a marker, surrounding turns shown but dimmed.

ALL TYPES: Clean metadata header — title, type badge, project badge, date. Not raw text bleeding together. Source ID shown small for cross-referencing.

Requires: graph read --json (structured source content with typed entries), graph source-meta --json (edges, entities, linked beads). Frontend: type-aware renderer that switches layout based on source.type (note, session, conversation, status, docs, agent-run).
close_reason: Type-aware source rendering: notes as cards (yellow border), sessions as chat (user left/blue, assistant right/gray), turn highlighting from search click-through, connections panel showing edges. All content rendered as markdown via marked.js.

title: Auto-merge dispatcher — merge agent branch to master on DONE, record merge commit on bead
description: After agent exits with DONE decision, dispatcher runs git merge agent/<bead> into master (fast-forward or merge commit). Records merge commit hash on bead. Enables dependent beads to chain — each new worktree branches from updated master with all prior agent work. Merge failures become BLOCKED decisions for human review via the Trace View.
notes: commit: 67b83fa feat: auto-merge agent branches to master on DONE
close_reason: Dispatcher auto-merges agent/<bead> to master on DONE. Records merge commit on bead. Merge conflicts abort and mark bead BLOCKED. Branch deleted after successful merge.

title: Git worktree lifecycle — dispatcher creates/mounts/validates/cleans up agent worktrees
description: Dispatcher creates a git worktree before agent launch, mounts it read-write into the container. Agent edits and commits normally. Dispatcher validates after exit (decision.json, diff sanity), records commit hash on bead, cleans up worktree. Replaces current broken model where repo is mounted :ro and agent output goes nowhere.
notes: commit: 615d399 feat: git worktree lifecycle for agent dispatch
close_reason: launch.sh creates git worktree, mounts read-write, agent commits normally. Dispatcher reads .commit_hash from output dir, records on bead, cleans up worktree. Tool guidelines updated to tell agent about /workspace/repo.

title: Layered container images — base + per-project images with dependency routing
description: Container image hierarchy: base (autonomy-agent) with claude+graph+bd, extended by project images (autonomy-agent:dashboard etc) that bake in project-specific deps. Dispatcher routes beads to the right image via LABEL_IMAGE_MAP keyed on bead labels. Agents can spin up servers, run tests, curl endpoints inside the container. Built: agents/images/dashboard/Dockerfile, launch.sh --image flag, dispatcher image_for_bead() routing.
notes: commit: 0d60512 feat: layered container images — per-project routing in dispatcher
close_reason: Built: agents/images/dashboard/Dockerfile extends base with starlette+uvicorn. launch.sh accepts --image flag. Dispatcher LABEL_IMAGE_MAP routes beads to right image by label. Verified: agent can start uvicorn, curl localhost, validate work inside container.

title: Live JSONL viewer — tail any session file and render assistant output in dashboard
description: Dashboard endpoint: /api/session/{id}/tail?after=N returns new entries from a session JSONL after byte offset N. Frontend: polls every 2-3s, renders new assistant text turns as markdown, auto-scrolls. Works for ALL session types: interactive (this session), subagents (tool-results/), container agents, background tasks. No tmux attachment needed — pure file tailing. The /sessions page shows active sessions; clicking one opens the live viewer. This is the jira web viewer live-tail pattern adapted for our dashboard.

title: Chat With integration — select any node, launch primed agent session
description: Button on every navigable view (source, bead, search result, entity). Clicking it: (1) generates a primer from the current node via graph primer, (2) launches a Claude session in the web terminal with that primer as initial context, (3) links the new session back to the source node. The agent starts with full context — no manual priming needed. Depends on: web terminal (done), primer generator (done), clickable navigation (this chain).

title: Source detail view — full content with connection sidebar
description: When viewing a source (/source/{id}), show: rendered markdown content (turns), plus a sidebar with all connections — edges to/from this source (implemented_by, conceived_at, spawned_by, responds_to), related entities, linked beads. Each connection is clickable, navigating to the linked node. This is the graph NAVIGATOR — not just reading content but traversing the knowledge graph.

title: graph search --json — structured output for dashboard consumption
description: Add --json flag to graph search CLI. Returns array of {id, source_id, source_title, project, type (thought/derivation), turn_number, content, rank}. The dashboard API currently returns raw text because search has no JSON mode. This is the foundation for all dashboard search rendering.
close_reason: graph search --json implemented. Returns array of {id, source_id, source_title, project, result_type, turn_number, content, rank}. Dashboard API updated to use --json. Frontend renders structured clickable cards with type badge, project tag, content preview. Click navigates to /source/{id}.

title: Clickable search results — navigate from search hit to source detail
description: Dashboard search results rendered as cards with: source title, project badge, content preview, turn number. Clicking a result navigates to /source/{id} which renders the full source. Each result shows its source_id for cross-referencing. Depends on graph search --json.

title: xterm.js WebSocket integration — PTY↔browser bridge in Starlette
description: Add WebSocket endpoint to dashboard server. Server-side: use pty.fork() or asyncio subprocess to spawn a shell/claude process, pipe stdin/stdout over WebSocket. Client-side: xterm.js terminal component connecting to ws://host:8080/ws/terminal. This is the core plumbing — everything else builds on it.
close_reason: xterm.js + WebSocket + PTY bridge implemented. Server: pty.fork() with asyncio read loop, stdin/stdout over WebSocket, terminal resize handling. Client: xterm.js with fit addon, auto-resize, reconnect status. Routes: /terminal page, /ws/terminal WebSocket. Supports: cmd param (any command), attach param (tmux session). Buttons: New Claude, New Bash.

title: Chat With button — spawn context-primed Claude session from any view
description: Button on bead detail, source view, search results. On click: generate primer for the current context (graph primer <bead_id> or compose from current view), spawn a new Claude session with that primer as the initial prompt, connect to it via the web terminal. Session is tracked in graph with provenance link to what spawned it.

title: Web terminal — embed interactive Claude sessions in dashboard via xterm.js + WebSocket
description: The core plumbing for all interactive dashboard features. Embed xterm.js in the dashboard connected via WebSocket to a server-side PTY. The PTY runs claude (or attaches to an existing tmux session). This gives: full interactive Claude in the browser, live streaming output, keyboard input, permission approval by typing, /btw messaging — everything. Technical path: (1) Add WebSocket endpoint to Starlette server, (2) Server-side: spawn PTY or tmux attach, pipe stdin/stdout over WS, (3) Client-side: xterm.js terminal component in dashboard, (4) 'Chat With' button spawns new PTY with context primer as initial prompt. Replaces /remote-control entirely. Accessible from phone over tailnet.

title: Chat With — launch contextual agent sessions from any dashboard view
description: Top-level dashboard feature: from any view (bead, source, search result, session), click 'Chat With' to launch a claude/codex instance with a context primer built from the current view. The primer is generated dynamically from the graph. The chat session is tracked, linked to the source bead/content, and observable in the dashboard. This is the bridge between passive viewing and active agent interaction. Encompasses: bead-scoped chats, source-scoped chats, graph-scoped research sessions. Each chat links back to what spawned it.

title: Dispatcher loop — deterministic claim/release/close for agent work
description: Deterministic Python loop that owns all bead state mutations. Agents run bd --readonly. Dispatcher does: query ready beads, filter claimed, claim via set-state, launch agent, wait for decision file, validate, update bead state. ~200 lines Python, no LLM in the loop. Conceived in session src:d15ebbae-df4.
notes: commit: ee4a4e7 (included in agent dispatch infrastructure commit by dashboard agent)
close_reason: agents/dispatcher.py built and tested. Deterministic loop: query ready beads by label, filter claimed, claim via set-state, launch agent container, collect decision.json, update bead state. Supports --loop, --dry-run, --queue. Verified against refinement queue (5 beads found).

title: Fix mid-work human message ingestion — sovereign content being discarded
description: When user sends a message while agent is running tools, Claude Code embeds it in a system-reminder tag inside an assistant tool_use entry. Our parser: (1) skips tool_use entries, (2) strips system-reminder tags. Result: human messages sent mid-work are silently lost. These are the MOST important messages — course corrections, pivotal decisions. Fix parse_claude_code_session() to extract 'user sent a new message while working' content and ingest as sovereign thoughts.
close_reason: Fixed: queue-operation entries now ingested as sovereign thoughts. graph attention command shows human input chronologically with [queued] tags. Re-ingest captured 65 previously-invisible mid-work messages from this session.

title: AgentRun interface — transport-agnostic lifecycle for agent capture and linking
description: Define the core AgentRun dataclass: bead_id, agent_id, prompt, trace_path, status, result. Implement ingest_agent_run() that: parses trace JSONL (regardless of source), creates a source linked to the bead, inserts thoughts/derivations, creates implemented_by edge. Same interface for subagent tool-results/ and docker cp output. This is the foundation all capture builds on.
close_reason: AgentRun interface implemented in tools/graph/agent_runs.py. parse_agent_trace() handles JSONL from both tool-results/ and /tmp/tasks/. ingest_agent_run() creates source, thoughts, derivations, entities, and cross-reference edges (spawned_by, implemented_by). discover_subagent_traces() finds traces in both locations. CLI: graph agent-runs [--list] [--session ID]. 87 subagent sessions ingested on first run.

title: Ingest subagent traces — tool-results/ JSONL files into graph
description: Claude Code stores subagent (sidechain) traces in tool-results/*.txt as JSONL, not in the main session JSONL. Our ingestion pipeline misses them entirely. Need to: discover tool-results/ dirs, parse each file as a subagent session, link to parent session, ingest thoughts/derivations. This is blocking all subagent observability — evals, research agents, code fix agents are all invisible.
close_reason: Covered by AgentRun interface — discover_subagent_traces() finds all tool-results/*.txt and /tmp/tasks/*.output files, filters to actual JSONL agent traces, ingests via the same pipeline. 87 traces ingested.

title: Fix graph search query escaping — FTS5 chokes on hyphens, special chars
description: FTS5 interprets hyphens as column operators: 'one-time' becomes 'column one minus time'. Queries with hyphens, colons, parentheses all break. Need to sanitize/quote user queries before passing to FTS5 MATCH. Also consider OR mode vs AND mode — current AND-only means 'scraper login auth session cookie' returns nothing because no single record has all 5 words. Should support both modes. This is blocking agent search efficiency right now.
close_reason: Fixed by Sonnet subagent in 56s. _sanitize_fts_query() wraps terms in quotes, strips operators, supports --or mode. Verified: hyphens no longer crash, multi-term OR queries work.

title: First autonomous bead execution — end-to-end proof of concept
description: Pick a real bead from the ready queue, dispatch it to a container, let it run to completion, collect results, verify bead was closed. The bootstrap moment.
close_reason: First autonomous execution complete. Agent dispatched on auto-emh, ran for ~8 minutes, built readiness.py + dispatcher integration + tests + primer changes. Decision file path mismatch fixed for next run. Output captured via --print stdout. Bootstrap moment achieved.

title: Agent launch script — docker run with env injection and bead context
description: Shell script: takes a bead ID, runs bd show to get context, launches container with Claude auth token, GRAPH_SCOPE, bead description as the prompt. Uses --dangerously-skip-permissions. Mounts repo + databases.
notes: commit: ee4a4e7 (included in agent dispatch infrastructure commit by dashboard agent)
close_reason: agents/launch.sh built and tested. Generates composed prompt, launches container with mounted DBs (readonly), collects decision.json. Dry-run verified.

title: Agent container image — Ubuntu + Claude Code + bd + graph + python
description: Dockerfile that bakes in: claude CLI, bd CLI, graph CLI, python3.12 + .venv with graph deps. Should start in <5s. No services, no DinD. Just a clean agent workspace.
notes: commit: ee4a4e7 (included in agent dispatch infrastructure commit by dashboard agent)
close_reason: Dockerfile built and tested. All tools verified: claude 2.1.76, bd, graph. Image 766MB, builds in ~20s. agents/Dockerfile + agents/build.sh

title: Sprint 1: Eyes and Hands — visibility + first agent dispatch
description: Three parallel tracks: (A) Dashboard for operational visibility, (B) Foundation tooling so agents can use bd+graph, (C) Container dispatch so agents can run autonomously. Converges to: first bead executed by an autonomous agent, observable through the dashboard.

title: Dashboard skeleton — Starlette server, Tailwind, static file serving
description: Minimal Starlette app with uvicorn, Tailwind CDN, vanilla JS. Serves on localhost:8080. Hot reload for templates. Base layout with nav sidebar.
notes: Deprioritized: beads-ui covers bead board. Our dashboard skeleton should focus on what beads-ui DOESN'T have: graph explorer, session monitor, markdown viewer, annotations. Still needed but the scope narrowed.
close_reason: Dashboard skeleton live at 0.0.0.0:8080. Starlette + Tailwind + marked.js. API: beads, search, sources, context, attention, primer, active sessions. Client-side routing, markdown rendering, project scope picker. Active session detection with 5s auto-refresh.

title: Trace View — bead to dispatch to session to result chain
description:
The Dispatch page is the single view for the full agent work lifecycle:

**Active Dispatches (top section):**
- Each dispatch shows: assigned bead (ID, title, priority), container status, runtime clock
- Click into a running dispatch → live session view (stream agent's conversation/tool use)
- Future: live diff view showing files changed in real-time

**Last Runs (bottom section, timeline):**
- Completed dispatches drop here with quick status indicators:
  - Did the agent close the bead? (DONE / BLOCKED / FAILED)
  - Did the merge succeed? (merged / conflict / no commits)
  - Did tests pass? (TBD)
- Ordered by completion time, most recent first

**Trace View (click on finished dispatch):**
- Full context: bead description, acceptance criteria, provenance links
- Composed prompt that was sent to the agent
- Session log: agent's full conversation and tool use
- Git diff: what changed on the agent's branch
- Decision.json: agent's self-assessment
- Experience report: operational feedback
- Graph notes: any trail markers dropped during execution
- Discovered beads: new work the agent identified

Data sources: bd show --json, git diff master..agent/<bead>, git log agent/<bead>, data/agent-runs/<bead>-<ts>/, graph read <session-source>.

title: Bead Board view — ready queue, dependency DAG, status lanes
description: Shows bd ready output, clickable beads with full detail, dependency graph visualization (simple DAG or tree), status columns (ready/in-progress/blocked/done). Calls bd ready --json, bd list --json, bd dep tree --json.
notes: beads-ui (community tool) running at http://100.122.70.30:3000 via tailnet. Renders all 32 beads, dependency tree, kanban view. Not mobile responsive. Accessible from phone. Consider this bead partially satisfied by beads-ui — our dashboard bead board can either wrap or replace it later.

title: Operational Dashboard — Web UI for beads, graph, sessions, agents
description: Starlette + Tailwind + vanilla JS dashboard. Bead board (ready queue, dependency DAG), session monitor (live agents, token burn), knowledge explorer (search, read, entities), playbook status, project overview, trace view. The operational visibility layer Jeremy has requested since session 1.

title: bd and graph CLIs permanently on PATH with agent permissions
description: Ensure bd and graph are in PATH for all sessions (bashrc). Add bd permission wildcards to settings.local.json. Verify both work from fresh shell.
close_reason: bd v0.60.0 and graph CLI both in PATH via ~/.local/bin wrappers. bd permissions added to settings.local.json. Verified from fresh shell.

title: Foundation: Beads + Graph Integration
description: Wire beads into the autonomy knowledge graph. bd commands available to agents via scoped wrappers. Session hooks capture bead state changes.

title: Agent Dispatch & Runtime
description: Spawn Claude Code and Codex instances from beads. Leverage GasTown agent registry patterns. tmux sessions with git worktree isolation. Support Claude Max subscription.

title: Autonomy Agentic Framework
description: Build the complete agentic development framework: beads-driven task tracking, agent dispatch, scoped graph access, result collection, and librarian automation. Bootstrap: use the system to build the system.

title: Migrate existing 'approved' beads to readiness:ready label
description:
The readiness gate replaces the 'approved' label with readiness:ready. Existing beads that have the 'approved' label need to be migrated: remove 'approved' label, run bd set-state <id> readiness=ready. A one-time migration script or bd query + loop would handle this.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All beads with 'approved' label get readiness:ready set via set-state
- 'approved' label is removed from migrated beads
- Dispatcher picks up migrated beads correctly

title: graph explore — single-command subgraph traversal with scatter control
description: A single graph command that starts from a query/node/label, traverses edges outward, and returns a condensed subgraph. Replaces dozens of manual search+read+show calls. Scatter parameter controls fan-out: tight (direct edges only), medium (one hop), wide (two hops). Output is structured: nodes, edges, key snippets. Generalizes what primer.py does for a single bead. Use cases: orient on a topic, explore before creating beads, feed context to subagents.

title: Entity/connection explorer — clickable graph traversal from any node
description: Given any node (source, bead, entity, thought), show all edges in/out. Click an edge to navigate to the other end. This is the recursive zoom: from a search result, to a source, to a linked bead, to the session that implemented it, to the turns where it was discussed. Every link is navigable. Needs: graph edges --json, graph entity --json.

title: Attach to existing session — drop into running agent via tmux attach
description: On the active sessions list, click a session to attach to it. Server-side: tmux attach-session -t <session> piped through the WebSocket PTY bridge. Read-only mode by default, with a toggle to enable input. This replaces /remote-control and tmux attach from the CLI.

title: Inline bead annotation — select markdown, leave comments for async agent reflection
description: Select text in a bead description or source view, leave a comment. The comment is stored as an annotation in the graph. A refinement agent can pick up annotations async, reflect on them, and integrate changes back into the bead. This is the paragraph-level annotation system applied to beads. Enables: human feedback on agent-written specs, iterative refinement without a live session, review comments that agents act on later.

title: Bead visualization upgrade — show closed beads, leverage beads-ui ecosystem
description: Current dashboard hides closed beads. Need: toggleable closed/open filter, timeline view, completion progress per epic. The beads community has extensive UI tooling (beads-ui, Foolery, BeadBoard, beads-web). Evaluate integrating or embedding rather than building from scratch. See COMMUNITY_TOOLS.md in the beads repo.

title: Live session viewer — stream agent output in dashboard with auto-scroll
description: Embed a live view of an active agent session in the dashboard. Tail the JSONL file, parse assistant text turns, render as markdown in a scrolling panel. Like the jira web viewer's live-tail but in our dashboard. Clicking an active session on /sessions opens this view.

title: Permission approval queue — approve/deny agent tool calls from dashboard
description: When an agent is blocked on a permission prompt, surface it in the dashboard with approve/deny buttons. Agent sessions running without --dangerously-skip-permissions would show pending approvals here. Requires: detecting permission prompts in the JSONL stream, a mechanism to send the approval back (tmux send-keys or stdin injection).

title: Label-based dispatch queues — route refinement/implementation/research to agent types
description: Dispatcher runs multiple queues via bd query on labels. Each queue routes to a different agent type with different prompt templates and capabilities. Refinement agents: graph read + bead propose. Implementation agents: worktree + bd readonly. Research agents: web + graph write. Labels define routing; beads carry the universal backlog. Conceived in session src:d15ebbae-df4.

title: Bead readiness gate — draft/spec-complete/ready lifecycle before dispatch
description: Add readiness dimension via set-state: draft -> spec-complete -> ready. Dispatcher only picks up readiness=ready beads. bd ready currently means no dependency blockers, not well-specified. bd lint checks sections; readiness gate formalizes the bar. Conceived in session src:d15ebbae-df4.
notes:
commit: d3112b5de4abded4bdc8b41b5ffcf0eab3a3ca9e branch: agent/auto-emh
Readiness gate replaces the 'approved' label. Dispatcher now queries for label=readiness:ready instead of label=approved. The readiness module provides: check_spec_complete() for automated validation, can_transition() for lifecycle enforcement, promote() for CLI-driven advancement, and filter_dispatch_ready() for dispatcher integration. 42 unit tests cover transitions, spec checks, filtering, and promotion. Existing beads with 'approved' labels will need migration to readiness:ready labels.
merged: 719547ef109cf1d3130586d42400b4a1af643d32
Failed: No decision file. Exit code: 128
Readiness gate is now enforced in dispatcher step 3. Beads without readiness:ready are skipped with a log message. Use `bd set-state <bead> readiness=ready` to mark beads for dispatch. The readiness module also provides a CLI: `python -m agents.readiness check|promote|set|gaps <bead-id>`.
close_reason: Implemented readiness gate with draft/spec-complete/ready lifecycle. Dispatcher now filters on readiness:ready label before dispatch.

title: Document sections as graph nodes — heading-level addressability and typed edges
description: Extend graph to ingest markdown docs with section-level granularity. Headings become addressable nodes like conversation turns. Typed edges between sections: satisfies, requires, supersedes, informs. Files remain as serialization for harness compatibility but graph holds the real structure. Conceived in session src:d15ebbae-df4.

title: Refinement pipeline — async capture-to-bead crystallization
description: Mid-conversation tagging of turns that need capture. Refinement agent picks up tags async: reads surrounding conversation context, searches graph for related knowledge, checks existing beads for overlap via find-duplicates, creates/updates properly specified and linked beads. Conceived in session src:d15ebbae-df4.

title: Bead creation with provenance — link to source turns at birth not death
description: Every bead should carry conceived_at provenance from the moment it's created. graph bead command wraps bd create + graph link in one step. Future: auto-detect current session and recent turns to suggest provenance links.

title: Bead-session edge linking — implemented_by, researched_by, informed_by relations
description: Extend graph edges to support bead↔session associations. Relations: implemented_by (bead→session that did the work), researched_by (bead→research session), informed_by (bead→source that provided context), spawned (parent session→subagent session). Enable graph queries like 'show all sessions for this bead' and 'what bead drove this session'.
close_reason: graph link already supports these relations. Tool guidelines instruct agents to use graph link. Automatic linking deferred to post-MVP.

title: Agent prompt composition — AGENTS.md + bead context + shared blocks
description: Composes the full agent prompt from: (1) dynamic context primer from auto-lks (graph-generated, bead-specific), (2) shared instruction blocks from agents/shared/ (tool guidelines, experience report schema, working style), (3) the specific task description. The primer replaces static CLAUDE.md for subagents. Shared blocks are the only static content — and even those should eventually be graph-generated. Depends on auto-lks for the primer generator.
notes: commit: ee4a4e7 (included in agent dispatch infrastructure commit by dashboard agent)
close_reason: Prompt composer built: agents/compose.py + agents/shared/{tool_guidelines,experience_report}.md. Tested against auto-5kj — generates 6.5KB prompt with primer + shared blocks + dispatcher directives.

title: Remote session observation — alternatives to /remote-control for monitoring agent sessions
description: Anthropic's /remote-control routes through their servers, too slow for permission approvals. Research: tmux attach for local, ttyd for web terminal, dashboard live-tail for polling, approval queue in dashboard UI. Short term: tmux. Medium term: dashboard. Long term: ACP (Agent Communication Protocol) from GasTown.

title: Markdown viewer — render graph content and session transcripts in browser
description: marked.js client-side rendering. Source reader view (graph read rendered as HTML). Session transcript viewer. Code syntax highlighting via highlight.js.

title: Session log review tooling — parse JSONL, compute tool use stats, flag issues
description: CLI command that analyzes a Claude Code session JSONL: tool call counts, search query success rate, repeated similar queries, time per tool, token efficiency. Feeds into prompt refinement.

title: Agent result collection — structured output from container to graph
description: When agent container exits: extract output files, parse structured result (completion status, tool feedback, discovered work), ingest into graph, update bead status via bd close/update.

title: Implement semantic vector search — sqlite-vec + nomic-embed + RRF hybrid
description: Research complete (musings/research-search-upgrade.md). Add sqlite-vec shadow table, embed 92K records with nomic-embed-text-v1.5 (384-dim Matryoshka), RRF fusion with FTS5, FlashRank reranker. Estimated: 2-3 days. Cuts agent query count 5-10x.

title: Knowledge Explorer — search, source reader, entity browser
description: Search box that calls graph search. Results link to full source reader (graph read). Entity browser showing mention counts and cross-references. Project filter dropdown.

title: Session Monitor — live and recent agent sessions
description: Shows active and recent Claude Code sessions from graph. Token consumption, wall time, turn count. For live sessions (JSONL still growing), show latest turn with auto-refresh. Calls graph sources --type session --project X.

title: Bridge: graph CLI can query bead status, bd can reference graph sources
description: graph beads command that calls bd ready/list/show. Lets agents use one tool to see both knowledge and work items. Could also mean ingesting bead state into the graph for search.
notes: Scope expanded: not just CLI bridge but the full association layer. Edges between beads (Dolt) and graph sources (SQLite). Relations: implemented_by, researched_by, informed_by, spawned. Critical for audit trail and discovery. See session discussion on bead-session linking.

title: Scoped bd wrappers — agents get project-locked bead access
description: Like graph-scope for GRAPH_SCOPE, create bd wrappers that lock agents to specific bead prefixes or databases. Agent runs bd ready and only sees beads in its scope.

title: bd prime integration — inject graph context alongside bead context
description: NOT just bd prime output bolted onto a static file. This is a dynamic context primer GENERATED FROM THE GRAPH at agent dispatch time. Given a bead ID, query the graph for: (1) bead description + acceptance criteria from bd show, (2) conceived_at provenance turns — the original conversation, (3) related graph notes tagged with relevant topics, (4) pitfall notes for tools the agent will use, (5) recent related beads in the same epic, (6) relevant tool documentation from docs sources. Output: a markdown primer that gives the agent everything it needs to start work, assembled on the fly. No static CLAUDE.md. The graph is the source of truth, the primer is a view.
close_reason: Implemented in tools/graph/primer.py. generate_primer() queries graph for: bead details, provenance turns, related notes, pitfalls, sibling beads, tool docs. CLI: graph primer <bead_id>. Tested against auto-a68 — pulls full provenance chain, all pitfalls, related architecture notes. This is the dynamic context generator that replaces static CLAUDE.md for agents.

title: Agent instructions file — AGENTS.md with bd+graph workflow
description: NOT a static file that agents read. This is the TEMPLATE for the shared instruction blocks that get composed into agent prompts dynamically. Defines: how to use bd and graph tools, what output format to produce (experience_report.md), working style guidelines. These blocks are assembled by the prompt composer (auto-coa), not read from disk by the agent. The agent never sees AGENTS.md — it sees a dynamically generated primer that includes these blocks.
close_reason: Superseded by dynamic prompt composition (auto-coa). Shared instruction blocks live in agents/shared/. No static AGENTS.md needed.

title: Focus: Abstract Work Orchestrator
description: The meta-layer that manages all autonomous work. Priority queue, environment registry, dispatch strategies, monitoring, tracing, cost tracking. Sits above beads and agent dispatch.

title: Non-Code Result Collection
description: GasTown collects results as git commits. We need result collection for research, playbooks, graph updates, and other non-code outputs. Structured output format, validation, graph ingestion.

title: Linux Agent Isolation
description: Solve the container/sandbox gap on Linux. GasTown uses macOS sandbox-exec. We need DinD, namespaces, seccomp, or E2B for equivalent isolation. Evaluate cost/complexity tradeoffs.

title: Worktree/session resumption — re-dispatch continues from prior agent branch
description: When re-dispatching a bead, detect if agent/<bead> branch exists with prior commits. Create worktree from that branch instead of master so the agent can continue where the previous run left off. Enables retry-after-failure and incremental work across multiple dispatch sessions.

title: Auto-set readiness=draft on bead creation
description:
New beads should automatically get readiness=draft set when created, so they enter the readiness lifecycle from the start. This could be a bd hook or a dispatcher pre-processing step.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every newly created bead gets readiness:draft label
- Works via bd create hook or post-creation automation
- No manual intervention needed

title: Session messaging — send /btw messages to running agents from dashboard
description: Inject messages into a running Claude Code session from the dashboard UI. Claude Code supports /btw for background messages. The dashboard would write to the session's stdin or use the queue-operation mechanism. Enables human-in-the-loop guidance without switching to the TUI.

title: Harness-to-graph bridge — agents edit files but knowledge lives in DB
description: Design tension: agent harnesses (Claude Code, Codex) natively edit files. We want knowledge in the graph DB. Options: (1) bidirectional sync files <-> graph sections, (2) CLI agents use instead of file edits. Need to resolve how agents author structured knowledge when their tools assume filesystem. Conceived in session src:d15ebbae-df4.

title: Experience report parsing — extract structured feedback from agent output
description: Parser for experience_report.md (design in musings/design-agent-observability.md). Extracts: completion status, confidence, tool experience, failed queries, discovered beads, prompt issues. Feeds into analytics pipeline. Part of the AgentRun result processing.

title: Annotation interface — comment on paragraphs in past sessions
description: Paragraph-level annotations on session content. Click a paragraph, add a comment. Stored in graph as annotation source type. Cross-session references.

title: Unified secret management — encrypted store, container injection, rotation tracking
description: Research complete (musings/research-secret-management.md). Covers Claude auth, Anthropic API key, GitHub tokens, Slack tokens, mesh certs. Phase 1: docker env vars. Phase 2: infra-style asymmetric encryption with single master password. Phase 3: focus secrets inject.

title: Session hooks — auto-sync beads on session start and pre-compact
description: SessionStart hook runs bd prime + graph sessions. PreCompact hook captures bead state. Ensures continuity across context compaction and new sessions.

title: Ingest bead state into knowledge graph for cross-reference
description: Periodically or on-change, export bd list --json and ingest into graph as a beads source type. Enables graph search to find related beads, and beads to reference graph sources.

title: Autonomy Model Alignment
description: Orient the framework around the Autonomy sovereignty model. Self-hosted, user-sovereign, scoped access. Mesh networking, internal DNS, secure agent environments. GRAPH_SCOPE enforcement.

title: Librarian Agents
description: Continuously running agents that maintain knowledge quality. Goal tracker (sessions→beads), playbook librarian (sessions→playbooks), spec validator (specs↔implementations), session annotator.

title: Auto-promote readiness when bd lint passes and labels present
description: Currently readiness must be set manually via set-state. Could add an auto-promote step that checks bd lint + readiness criteria and auto-sets readiness:spec-complete or readiness:ready when all criteria are met. Would reduce manual workflow overhead.

title: Add readiness column to bd list and bd ready output
description: The readiness level is stored as a label (readiness:draft/spec-complete/ready) but bd list doesn't show it in a dedicated column. Adding a readiness column would make it visible at a glance which beads are ready for dispatch vs still being refined.
